entry.title: Re: onSelect script: How to read dimension property value...
entry.content: Amazing it is working as you suggested!! I used getResultMember() instead of getResultSet() — I was trying to get a single member, but getResultSet() is the correct way to get the full row data including properties. I tried to access properties directly as member.properties["ExtLink"] — The correct path is deeper: member[0]["Story"].properties["Story.ExtLink"]. I missed both the array index [0], the dimension name ["Story"], and the fact that the property key needs the dimension prefix "Story.ExtLink" not just "ExtLink".

entry.title: Re: Trying to reconcile CO09 with BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY
entry.content: On one of my questions, I had accepted one answer, and I might unaccept as follows:

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors AI - AI-Assisted Performance Insights for Manager (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
@jenny_geipel 

Do you know how to enable this feature after forms have been launched?

Also, you mention the form must be with a manager in the single-role step and in there Inbox. We have multiple steps when the form gets back to the manager for review, will the option be at each of these steps or just at the initial manager step?

TIA, Lynn

entry.title: Re: RAP : Multi Level List (3 level)
entry.content: Hi ruchirsaxena, can you give details on how did you resolve this issue? I'm currently having the same problem

entry.title: Re: Accelerate Insights with the new Quick Builder in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@MXK ,

Structure is supported in the Quick Builder. You are right — this is another advantage compared to the Navigation Panel. In the Quick Builder, you can select different structure members, but unlike the Design‑Time Builder Panel, you cannot create new structure members there.

Regarding your case (screenshot above), I am not able to reproduce the issue in my internal tenant. Please create a support ticket  so that we can investigate further. In particular, we need to understand how your model is defined.

thank you very much for your comment

Jie Deng

entry.title: Where did the BTP ABAP certification (C_ABAPD) go?
entry.content: I've noticed for the past week that when I go to the SAP certifications page, I can't find the BTP ABAP certification ("SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud") listed anywhere.  I realize SAP was going to convert it to the new practical test format, but I still see other certifications listed that are being converted to that format as well.  Will C_ABAPD be added back to the certifications page soon? SAP BTP ABAP environment Certification

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP S/4HANA from 2021 to 2023 FPS02: Wrong Fi...
entry.content:
Hello, 

I suspect the SUM had an issue with the maintenance created on Maintenance Planner. You can check this in the SUM logs:

If this is found SUM stops and ask to choose which options you'd like to choose...

Deviation is listed where it thinks cannot reach the predefined target.

All the best

entry.title: Re: What's New in Supply Chain in SAP Cloud ERP 2602?
entry.content: Very nice 🙂

entry.title: Re: Clear contents of Control.Type.FormCell.Attachment is...
entry.content:
Assuming you do not set any initial value for the control when the page loads I would suggest using the reset() method.  Please note you should be using the evaluateTargetPathForAPI to get the proxy call reference or use getControl to get the reference to the control.

context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:UploadAgreement/#Control:FormCellAttachment").reset();

entry.title: Re: Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launchpad Pages"
entry.content: Hello i have the same issue , i added the fiori ID in ADT and problem is not resolved.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – UX Adoption accelerators updated for AI, Clean Core, and S/4HANA 202
entry.content: Great stuff, thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2025: Key Innovations in Sales for the Cloud Private Edition
entry.content: Wow, a lot of cool stuff!

entry.title: Re: Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launchpad Pages"
entry.content: Hello i had the same issue, i added the fiori ID and the problem is not resolved. in my custom business catalog , i can se the custom catalog extension holding my iam app but when i click on it it says the requested is not found. could you please help ?

entry.title: Re: IC Stock Transfer Returns Process w/ outbound and inbound delivery (Private Cloud / On Prem)
entry.content:
Hi @kulsar_007 
you can activate NB for the return as well, but maybe you have different output requirements than for regular PO type. Just food for though.

regarding the other question, I follow here the standard setup (item categories):
the returning site creates a outbound delivery with item category NLLR and movement type 161.
When this is posted goods issue, the stock is in transit and with output type SPED an inbound delivery with item category ELCR and movement type 673 is created. So far, the stock is not received. Only after goods receipt of the inbound delivery, the stock is available.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.
entry.content: Screenshot attached

entry.title: Custom Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launchpad Pages"
entry.content:
Hello,

I am facing an issue deploying my app in S/4HANA Public Cloud. I developed it using RAP and Fiori in BAS, following this tutorial:
https://sapzero2hero.com/2024/01/21/sap-s-4hana-cloud-developer-extensibility-with-upload-excel/

I cannot see the business catalog in Manage Launchpad Pages. In my custom catalog, the Custom Catalog Extension contains the IAM app created in ADT (Eclipse), but when I click it, I get “The requested catalog was not found".

However, I can see the IAM app in Display IAM Apps.

Could you please help ?

entry.title: Re: Trying to reconcile CO09 with BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY
entry.content: Got it. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Where did the BTP ABAP certification (C_ABAPD) go?
entry.content: Yes, they are in the process of refining the exam and validation rules now. Stay tuned for more information coming soon.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.
entry.content:
It is in the 'My Learning' page, under the sub heading 'Teams'.

Screenshot attached.

entry.title: Rework Production order, its component level price distribution from Purchased material.
entry.content:
There is requirement to dismantle the product into its component that becomes saleable product for the business. The component which is received through 531 movement types, are getting sale through creating sale order. 

The business wants that the price of Product which has been purchase from outside at Moving average price will go under dismantling and its price  should be distributed percentage wise to its several components materials assign in the BOM of hypothetical material code, Because of  Rework production order creation through CO07, how the price is distributed to its components material codes. Kindly suggest how it can be mapped in real time scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Using AS2 Context property AS2Filename in ICO
entry.content: Hi Could you please help as i am also facing the same issue where I have an issue related to two integrated configuration(ICO) when partner send .xml files with below filenaming conventions. Interface 1 : xxxx_APPROVED_xxxx.xml Interface 2 : Invoice_AP_XXXX.xml Interface 1 and 2 are configured with same AS2 sender channel with different message structures. when client triggers any of the AS2 message or payload to SAP PI 7.5 single stack, PI picks or selects the Interface/ICO which was last activated one. i need to configure both interfaces with same AS2 channel that gets the correct file ( depending on filenaming conventions ) to select correct interface/ICO accordingly. please suggest on how to resolve this issue step by step.

entry.title: Re: Request for SAP GUI for Java (macOS) Installation Pac...
entry.content:
The official SAP GUI for JAVA is here: https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap/trials-downloads.html?search=SAP%20GUI%20for%20Java

SAP GUI for Java provides native support for ARM architecture on macOS starting with version 7.80.

Look also into the SAP note: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3368441. To access this note, you do need an S-USER, but to download the software, you just need to create a account using your business email address.

entry.title: Re: MDK offline app Sync offline data service failure Ass...
entry.content:
You probably have added an UploadCategory in the UploadOffline-Action without specifiying it.

Solution: Delete the empty UploadCategory:

entry.title: Re: Remote Debugging of SAP CAPM App - Nodejs
entry.content: For this to work, can I use SAP BAS in Browser or I need have local VSCode Installation?

entry.title: Re: Getting error while creating new Content Provider in Build Work Zone cockpit
entry.content:
Hello @Harshada 

We are facing this issue now.
Could you please let me know if you have the solution for this error.
It will be helpful for me.
Thanks

entry.title: SAP Data Reconciliation: Moving from Matching Maze to The Autonomous Future
entry.content:
BACKGROUND

There are two types of SAP reconciliation meetings during cutover.

Meeting Type 1 (What we planned):

“Let’s quickly confirm data moved correctly.”

Meeting Type 2 (What actually happens):

  • Finance says revenue looks off.
  • Procurement says suppliers are complaining.
  • Supply chain says inventory is inflated.
  • IT says record counts match.
  • Everyone stares at IT.

Welcome to the beautiful chaos of SAP data reconciliation.

Whether you’re:

  • Splitting a company
  • Running S/4 alongside ECC
  • Sharing data with subsidiaries
  • Migrating to a new instance
  • Or integrating systems after M&A

Data matching in SAP is not just technical data reconciliation. It’s business continuity surgery.

Why SAP Reconciliation Feels So Painful Today with current ETL tools?

Let’s be honest about the current state.

We Reconcile Tables, Not Decisions

We check:

  • Counts
  • Totals
  • Field matches

But business runs on:

  • Commitments
  • Revenue recognition
  • Inventory valuation
  • Working capital

Matching tables ≠ preserving business intent.

SAP Processes Are Chains, Not Records

Every transaction is part of a flow:

  • Forecast → Production → Inventory → Sale → Revenue
  • Contract → Order → Delivery → Billing → Cash
  • Budget → Commitment → Accrual → Payment

Break one link, and financial truth bends quietly.

Reconciliation today doesn’t understand chains. It understands spreadsheets.

Customization Is Everywhere

Every SAP system has:

  • Custom fields
  • Custom pricing logic
  • Custom movement types
  • Custom account determination
  • Custom approval flows

Generic reconciliation tools look at this and say: “Good luck.”

Real Business Reconciliation KPIs (Not Row Counts)

Let’s talk about reconciliation the way your CFO thinks about it.

Procure-to-Pay (P2P): The “Where Did My Money Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

PO Amount → GR Amount → Invoice Amount → Payment Amount

Real Business KPIs:

PO to GR Consumption Ratio

What % of PO value has been physically received?

  • Detects over-delivery or under-delivery
  • Identifies ghost POs
  • Highlights operational leakage
If PO is $1M and GR is $1.4M, someone is shopping.

GR to Invoice Variance %

Are vendors invoicing more than what was received?

  • Detects pricing issues
  • Catches duplicate invoicing
  • Flags freight/tax mismatches

This is not technical reconciliation. This is margin protection.

Order-to-Cash (O2C): The “Where Did My Revenue Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

Sales Order → Delivery → Billing → AR → Cash

Real Business KPIs:

Order Fulfillment Integrity

% of orders fully delivered as priced.

  • Detects partial deliveries not billed
  • Finds pricing condition errors
  • Highlights logistics-billing gaps

Delivery-to-Billing Variance

Does billing reflect delivered quantity and agreed price? This catches:

  • Wrong pricing conditions
  • Missed surcharges
  • Freight miscalculation

Revenue leakage hides here.

Manufacturing: The “Why Is My Warehouse Lying?” Flow

Business Flow:

Material → Production → Inventory → Valuation → COGS

Real Business KPIs:

Inventory Valuation Consistency

Does total inventory value match between systems by:

  • Plant
  • Material group
  • Valuation class

Mismatch here = financial statement distortion.

BOM Integrity Impact

% of production orders where:

  • Component consumption matches BOM
  • Standard cost matches actual cost

Incorrect BOM migration? It is cost explosion.

The Real Question for the Future

When we reshare data between SAP systems in the future, we should not ask:

“Did the data copy and tables and fields match?”

We should ask:

“Did the business outcome stay intact?”

That is a completely different problem. And it requires a completely different capability set.

The Future: Business-First, Agentic SAP Reconciliation

Let’s imagine what reconciliation should look like 5 years from now.

Not reactive. Not 400 dashboards..

But autonomous, business-aware, and proactive.

Flow-Centric Reconciliation

Instead of reconciling:

  • Material master
  • Vendor master
  • GL master

We reconcile:

  • Revenue lifecycle integrity
  • Working capital stability
  • Margin preservation
  • Inventory flow accuracy
  • Commitment-to-cash cycles

Example:

Instead of: “Material record count and fields matches.” The system says:

“Inventory valuation variance of 1.8% driven by missing moving average price adjustments in 3 plants. Financial impact: $2.1M.”

That’s business reconciliation.

Agentic Automation Capabilities of Future Data Reconciliation Tool

Here’s what real agentic SAP reconciliation must be capable of:

1. Business Context Modeling

The system must understand:

  • How revenue is recognized
  • How inventory is valued
  • How pricing conditions affect margin
  • How tax determination impacts compliance
  • How cost flows through production

Not just tables — but intent.

2. Cross-Process Correlation

If:

  • Billing increases
  • But cash conversion drops
  • And AR aging shifts

The system correlates these across modules. SD + FI + CO + Treasury. Today, that takes 3 teams and 2 weeks.

Future system: “Cash conversion delay traced to payment term misalignment for customers migrated from legacy region.”

3. KPI Stability Monitoring

When data is migration between SAP systems, the system should monitor:

  • Revenue per customer
  • Margin per product
  • Inventory turns
  • Working capital ratio
  • DSO / DPO shifts
  • Production yield

If KPIs deviate outside normal behavior bands. Alert triggered. Because business drift matters more than table drift.

4. Intelligent Variance Classification

Not every difference is a problem. Agentic reconciliation should classify:

  • Configuration difference
  • Timing difference
  • Master data gap
  • Transaction sequencing issue
  • True financial error

And suggest action.

Not just: “Mismatch found.”
But: “Difference due to delayed goods issue posting in target system. Expected to auto-correct in next batch.”

That reduces panic and so many meetings.

Executive-Ready Narrative

Future reconciliation shouldn’t produce:

  • 47 dashboards
  • 16 pivot graphs
  • A nervous data analyst

It should produce:

“Post SAP data migration, revenue and margin remain stable within historical variance bands. Minor inventory timing differences detected in 2 plants with no financial impact. No systemic control breaks identified.”

That’s board ready confidence.

The Big Shift: From Data Movement to Business Assurance

Old model:

Move data. Validate data. Sign off.

Future model:

Simulate business impact. Continuously assure process integrity. Autonomously detect drift. Explain root cause.

Reconciliation becomes less about matching numbers…and more about protecting enterprise behavior.

What Organizations Expect in Future SAP Reconciliations?

In the future, companies won’t ask: “How many records mismatched?” They’ll ask:

  • Are warehouse movements as expected?
  • Did margin integrity hold?
  • Did working capital spike?
  • Did revenue timing shift?
  • Did operational velocity slow down?
  • Did risk exposure increase?
  • Does financial demand and supply are working as expected?

That’s reconciliation redefined in the era of agentic automation!!

entry.title: Re: Understanding "My Processes" and "My Requests" in SBPA: Empowering Users Thr
entry.content:
I am running into an issue with the "My Processes" App in a shared environment.

When I share an environment with someone (with the ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection) and don't give them the Supervise authorization they are not able to see the processes they are involved in in that shared environment, however when I give them the Supervise authorization then they are able to see all of the processes from that environment in the "My Processes" app. Is that intended? Is there a way to give someone access to a shared environment in a way that they are able to only see the processes they are directly involved in? The "My Requests" App seems to work fine in that regard since it doesn't require the Supervise authorization but only the Execute authorization.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP S/4HANA from 2021 to 2023 FPS02: Wrong Fi...
entry.content:
Good Afternoon,

Thank you for your follow-up, even more than a year later 😊

As mentioned in my original post, the issue is not related to SUM.

The root cause lies within Maintenance Planner.
Maintenance Planner generated a stack that does not comply with SAP’s own recommendations because it selected a lower Support Package (SP) level for SAP Fiori Front-End Server for SAP S/4HANA 2023 than for the other SAP Fiori components.
This inconsistency in SP levels led to the issue.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: JSP compile error - java file name too long
entry.content: LongPathTool helps handle long file paths that cause these compiler issues.

entry.title: Re: Stay on top of regulatory changes in SAP Business Network (and more...)
entry.content: Happy to see this 🙂

entry.title: Re: Adaptation project: Override not being called/not wor...
entry.content:
The reason that changes are not applied when running on the ABAP system could be that the app variant is not started at all but the original app is started instead. This could be caused by incorrect target mappings, which maybe do not fit or are ambiguous.

The best way to check this is to check the network trace when starting the app. Here look for a file called manifest.appdescr_variant. Open its content and under sap.app/id, you will find what is really started.
If it does not match your project Id, your target mappings do not work.

entry.title: How Do VARs Continue to Offer L1/L2 Support After Migrating to SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
How do VARs continue to offer L1/L2 support to end customers after migrating to SAP Cloud ALM? And how can tickets be escalated to SAP if needed?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How Do VARs Continue to Offer L1/L2 Support After Mig...
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM does not include an IT Service Desk. If VARs wish to continue with L1/L2 ticket support, External API Management can be used to connect an SAP Cloud ALM tenant to any 3rd party service desk solutions.

That 3rd party ticketing tool can then use the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API to forward those tickets to the SAP Support Backbone. The goal of the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API is to support cases management from your own ITSM system. This API lets you integrate SAP cases manages in your incident and problem processes. Cases created/managed via the SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API are fully compatible with SAP standard support access points, i.e. SAP for Me, SAP for Me mobile app, Built-In Support.

3565170 - VAR case forwarding via SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API

Other similar questions and their answers regarding the transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM can be found here. For more information on Transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM, check the linked web page.

entry.title: Re: ATC Execution in HANA 2025 does not show any results
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for your response @BjoernJueliger .

After deep debugging we figured out that someone in the past commented some code in the class - CL_CI_TESTS which was not allowing the ATC tool to execute the code to run the checks. After uncommenting and bringing back the standard code, ATC works perfectly fine now.

Sorry for all the hassle.

entry.title: Re: IC Stock Transfer Returns Process w/ outbound and inbound delivery (Private Cloud / On Prem)
entry.content:
Thanks Ronald, 

Thanks for your reply. 

I have activated NB2C PO document type and able to trigger 161 movement.  Delivery type LLR / Item category is triggered correctly. I see confirmation control key also automatically coming on PO for creating Inbound delivery.  

The below part still not working for me. After good's issue I am using SPER output so in case if we reverse PGI then it should automatically cancel /delete Inbound delivery. 

I am unable to trigger Inbound delivery so far also need to check if its going to transfer  HU and serial number on Inbound delivery. 

When this is posted goods issue, the stock is in transit and with output type SPED an inbound delivery with item category ELCR and movement type 673 is created. So far, the stock is not received. Only after goods receipt of the inbound delivery, the stock is available.

 

I am missing something into partner profile set up.

entry.title: Re: IC Stock Transfer Returns Process w/ outbound and inbound delivery (Private Cloud / On Prem)
entry.content:
It would be great help if you could share partner profile setting so I will check from my side if I am missing any steps causing the issue and its not triggering O/P to create Inbound delivery in the background to perform Goods receit.

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Sarang Kulkarni

entry.title: Extensibility Guide for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Extensibility Guide for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 

 

The guide covers the following topics:

  • Extensibility Options
    • In-App Extensibility
    • Side-by-Side Extensibility
  • Low-Code/No-Code vs. Pro-Code
  • Clean Core Principles

Link to the document

 

More guidance can be found in the Onboarding Resource Center SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
Welcome to your one-stop-shop for all things onboarding! Explore SAP resources, step-by-step guides, assets, and join our webcast sessions to gain insights into your specific solution—ensuring a smooth and seamless onboarding experience.

 

Feel free to like or comment.. You can reach out to us anytime at SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
Your code is about reading the documents in MM02 (SAP component CA-DMS), but maybe the OP is talking about GOS attachments.

Also, you're only returning the image as XSTRING but for Smart Forms it's completely different you must create the image in SE78 dynamically. How to upload images in SE78 has been answered in older questions.

NB: for information, your code:

        "Get file data
        OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR INPUT IN BINARY MODE.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          DO.                                            "#EC CI_NESTED
            READ DATASET lv_file INTO ls_line.
            IF sy-subrc = 0.
              APPEND ls_line TO lt_file_data.
            ELSE.
              APPEND ls_line TO lt_file_data.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.
          ENDDO.
          CLOSE DATASET lv_file.
        ENDIF.

can be simplified to one read because because the file is opened with IN BINARY MODE and EV_STREAM is of type XSTRING, so this one READ will read the whole file:

        "Get file data
        OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR INPUT IN BINARY MODE.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          READ DATASET lv_file INTO ev_stream. "#EC CI_SUBRC
          CLOSE DATASET lv_file.
        ENDIF.

entry.title: 07.SAP SuccessFactors HCM Implementation - Top 10 Project Execution Risks and Mitigation Plan
entry.content:
This is a continuous blog series on the SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation. This blog explores the most common execution risks I have observed in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementations and outlines practical mitigation strategies based on my experience.

Over the course of my career, I have been involved in more than 40 SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation projects, serving roles ranging from consultant to architect, owning project deliverables, and working with partners to provide advisory guidance. Each project journey has been different, bringing unique challenges, cultural nuances, system landscapes, complex requirements, heavily customized processes, and business expectations.

While many assume that implementation risks primarily stem from product gaps or technical limitations, my experience tells a different story. In the vast majority of cases, project implementation challenges arise not from system limitations, but from human factors(errors) — decision delays, lack of skill, unclear ownership, communication gaps, misaligned expectations, or inadequate governance.

 

Every SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation begins with a strong intent:

 To eliminate legacy system challenges

  • To introduce a modern, sophisticated HCM platform
  • To standardize and streamline HR processes
  • To deliver within agreed timelines
  • To stay within the approved budget
  • Deliver high customer satisfaction and adoption

Yet despite clear objectives and executive sponsorship, some projects often face execution risks that threaten timelines, budgets, and stakeholder confidence.

 

Risk 1) Strategy and Plan in Place but No Tangible Outcomes Delivered :

 Risk Description: Although a clear strategy and a detailed plan were defined, and iterations were planned and executed accordingly, the expected outcomes were not achieved.

The implementation phase progressed smoothly up to System Integration Testing (SIT). However, during SIT and User Acceptance Testing (UAT), significant gaps were identified. These included newly emerging requirements, unresolved data quality issues, and incomplete system integrations. As a result, the project experienced delays, rework, and misalignment with expected deliverables.

“Creating a strategy is not complex, but execution and delivering the outcomes are the biggest challenges.”

Mitigation Plan:

This is a common risk in complex projects. To mitigate it:

  1. Define clear, measurable success criteria and expected outcomes for each project phase.
  2. Thoroughly validate requirements before moving into the build and testing phases.
  3. Every stage of the project must be evaluated based on measurable outcomes and value delivered — not merely on task completion or milestone achievement.
  4. Conduct early data profiling and integration validation to identify issues upfront.
  5. Introduce interim checkpoints focused on business outcomes rather than just task completion.
  6. Ensure stakeholder sign-off at each stage based on deliverable quality and readiness.
  7. Success Criteria for each Iteration, migration mock load, testing, integration build, etc.

Risk 2 ) Unskilled Key Resources from the System Integrator (SI):

Risk Description:  The customer selected SI to implement SAP SuccessFactors HCM following a thorough RFP process and validation. However, SI’s resources lack the skills, leading to major quality issues and failing to meet expected standards or deliver on promises. As a result, project quality declines and stakeholder confidence erodes.

"In my experience, this is one of the most critical risks in any SAP transformation program".

Mitigation Plan:

 Customers must proactively evaluate and validate the competency of the SI’s key resources before and during the project. The most critical roles to assess include: 

 

  • Project Manager/Delivery
  • Solution Architect
  • Module Leads
  • Technical Architect
  • Data Migration Lead

 

These are strategic SI roles that directly determine the success of the implementation. As a customer, you are entitled to demand the right skill set and experience level aligned with your transformation goals.

Example Context: SAP S/4HANA HCM to SAP SuccessFactors Transformation:

 For organizations transitioning from SAP S/4HANA HCM to SAP SuccessFactors HCM, cross-platform expertise is essential. The SI team must demonstrate proven experience in both on-premise HCM environments and cloud-based SuccessFactors architecture to ensure seamless integration, data integrity, and process continuity

Required Competency Profile for Key SI Roles

Risk 3) Resource Availability and Not Taking Ownership of Project Execution by the Customer

Risk Description: The notion that a System Integrator (SI) should fully drive the project and ensure successful delivery is a myth. While the SI plays a very critical role, the ultimate accountability for project success lies with the customer. Without strong customer ownership, project oversight often faces delays, scope creep, misalignment with business objectives, and poor adoption.

 

Mitigation Plan:

 Customers must take active ownership of project delivery by owning each work stream and customer leads take key roles and assign dedicated resouces:

  • Establishing a strong internal project sponsor and governance structure
  • Defining clear business objectives and success criteria
  • Allocating dedicated business and technical stakeholders
  • Making timely decisions and resolving escalations
  • Actively participating in requirement validation, testing, and change management
  • Monitoring progress through structured reviews and performance metrics
  • Prior to project initiation, confirm resource planning and allocation from the customer’s side.
  • Identify key business, technical, and business analyst resources, ensuring they are dedicated and available to work with the System Integrator (SI) throughout the project.
  • Customer Leads must own the deliverables and ensure that SI delivers them.
  • Create a detailed RACI and identify customer deliverables

For more detailed guidance, refer to the blog mentioned below.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/03-sap-successfactors-hcm-implementation-phase-0-customer-readiness-and/ba-p/14292649

Risk 4) Never-Ending Scope and Requirements (Scope Creep)

Risk Description: Scope creep is one of the biggest risk factors in project delivery. While the System Integrator (SI) may define the scope and set aggressive timelines at the beginning, continuous change requests during implementation can significantly extend delivery timelines, increase costs, and impact overall project quality.

Frequent change orders often become part of the implementation process, adding complexity and delaying milestones.

Mitigation Plan:

  • Conduct a thorough Phase 0 (Discovery/Planning Phase) to clearly define and validate requirements, and validate the scope defined by SI.
  • As a customer, review and analyze the scope multiple times to ensure it is accurately and comprehensively documented in the Statement of Work (SOW).
  • Establish strong governance and a formal Change Control Process.
  • Implement a structured approval mechanism for all change requests, including impact analysis (cost, timeline, resources, and go-live impact) before approval.
  • Maintain clear documentation and traceability of all scope changes.

Risk 5) Overselling and Aggressive Timelines by System Integrator (SI) :

 Risk Description: The consulting market is highly competitive, and overselling is a common practice to win deals. But this can lead to unrealistic expectations, project delays, increased costs, and failure to achieve expected outcomes.

Mitigation Plan:

  1. Validate proposed timelines and ensure they are realistic.
  2. Leverage your knowledge of your business processes, customizations, complexity, testing requirements, data, integrations, and organizational culture to assess feasibility.
  3. If timelines appear unrealistic, plan for an adequate buffer time, a minimum 20% extra time/Budget.
  4. Challenge the SI and request realistic data points to support the proposed schedule.

Risk 6) Treating the Project as a Technical Upgrade - No Transformation 

Risk Description: Approaching the project as merely a technical product update—such as replicating the legacy system on a different tech stack—overlooks the transformation potential of every SAP SuccessFactors implementation. This can result in missed opportunities to redefine processes and improve business outcomes. This will lead to not reaching business outcomes.

Mitigation Plan:

  1. Avoid treating the project purely as a technical upgrade; ensure business takes considerations drive key decisions.
  2. Maintain a balance between the business and technical teams,
    • Business team: Owns process redesign and defines requirements.
    • Technical team: Validates SI deliverables and ensures technical feasibility.
  3. Emphasize collaboration between business and tech teams throughout the implementation to prevent misalignment.

Risk 7)Underestimating the integration effort

 Risk Description: Integration efforts require substantial coordination, technical design, and joint ownership between the Customer and System Integrator (SI). Historically, integration scope and complexity are underestimated, and development is initiated too late in the delivery lifecycle.

 

When the integration work stream is not executed correctly, the project poses a high risk to the overall program's success.

Mitigation Plan:

 

  • Establish a comprehensive inventory of all inbound and outbound integrations at project initiation.
  • Classify each interface with a clear disposition strategy: Retain, Redesign, Retire, or Replace.
  • Confirm architecture principles and integration standards upfront.
  • Secure formal sign-off on the integration scope baseline.
  • Sequence integrations based on Business criticality, Technical complexity, and dependency on core object configuration.
  • Finalize the EC data model during Iteration 1 to 80-90% of fields. This enables early interface design , mapping and build
  • Initiate build activities for high-risk integrations as early as possible.
  • Integration success requires active customer engagement. The customer must assign committed resources .
  • Each interface must have a completed Testing Evidence Document.
  • Review the testing results and provide sign-off before moving to SIT.

Risk 8 ) Underestimating the Data Migration effort:

 Risk Description: Data migration is a critical success factor in any implementation project. Historically, many project delays are caused by underestimating the complexity, effort, and ownership required for data migration.

 Mitigation Plan:

 

  •  Clearly define the scope of data migration based on the customer’s current system and business requirements.
  •  Adopt a top-of-stack migration approach (migrate only necessary, current, and active data).
  • Limit historical data migration wherever possible. Load a maximum of one year of historical data unless legally or operationally required.
  • Know Your Data – Clean Before You Migrate. Perform detailed data profiling and analysis before mock loads.
  • Fix data quality issues in the source system before migration, and don’t wait to fix after Mock loads.
  • The customer must assign dedicated business and technical resources to provide requirements, own the work stream, validate data, and cleanse data.
  • Choose the right tools aligned with SAP's leading practice recommendations for data migration and validation.

Risk 9)Underestimating Testing  effort

 Risk Description: Testing takes significant effort, duration, and resources from the customer and SI.

Mitigation Plan:

 

  • Identify and document end-to-end test scenarios covering all business processes, integrations, and cross-module workflows as early as possible in the project lifecycle. This ensures complete process validation and minimizes gaps.
  • Utilize testing automation tools for regression testing and configuration validation. Automation improves efficiency, ensures repeatability, and reduces manual effort during multiple test cycles.
  • Perform detailed functional testing at the end of every iteration. Early validation of configurations and enhancements helps reduce last-minute surprises during User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
  • Validate inter-module integrations and process dependencies before executing full end-to-end testing. Ensuring that upstream and downstream processes work seamlessly reduces rework during later testing phases.
  • Allocate adequate resources for the testing workstream and provide structured training to the customer team on SAP SuccessFactors HCM. Empowering the client team ensures effective participation during System Integration Testing (SIT) and UAT.

 

Risk 10) Lack of transparency in Project status reporting and Risk identification.

Risk Description: There is a risk that project status reporting does not provide full transparency across all workstreams. Often, project reports indicate a positive status during earlier phases, but significant issues surface only at the System Integration Testing (SIT) stage.

The primary cause of this lack of transparency is insufficient workstream-level visibility and delayed risk escalation. As a customer, it is important to request and review progressively detailed updates throughout the project lifecycle to ensure early identification of potential issues.

Mitigation Plan:

  • Conduct regular status reviews for each workstream to ensure continuous visibility and alignment.
  • Track measurable outcomes, key deliverables, milestones, and cross-stream dependencies.
  • Establish clear ownership, defined roles, and accountability for each workstream.
  • Implement a structured risk management framework and maintain an active risk register, including impact assessment and mitigation tracking.
  • Promote a culture of early risk identification and escalation without penalty or blame.
  • Conduct retrospective meetings after every major milestone to evaluate performance and challenges.
  • Capture lessons learned and identify improvement opportunities.
  • Implement corrective and preventive actions to avoid recurrence of similar issues.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2025, Fully-Activated Appliance , Canada localization
entry.content:
Hello,

We recently created the SAP S/4HANA 2025, Fully-Activated Appliance. However the demo client 100 only has US best practices and Demo data.

We need both US and Canada Best practices in one single client. It is recommended to not activate Canada best practices in client 100. 

What are our options to achieve the requirement.

Need inputs

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: IC Stock Transfer Returns Process w/ outbound and inbound delivery (Private Cloud / On Prem)
entry.content: Also, I observed after activating return flag shipping point/Sales Org/DC/Division/Customer is not automatically changed.

entry.title: Re: Contingent Worker/External to Employee Conversions th...
entry.content: This is not a viable solution for us, and likely not for others as well. Our contingent workers typically end their assignment on Friday and return on Monday as employees. We enter the contingent worker termination on Sunday to ensure they do not lose IT access during the transition to employee status. Even if we were to terminate them on Saturday, that would leave only one day for HR to initiate onboarding, for the contingent worker to complete their tasks, and for HR to finalize onboarding as a new hire. During this gap, the worker would lose access and it's not feasible to complete all tasks in one day. In Onboarding 1.0, the system recognized future‑dated terminations and allowed workers to proceed with onboarding using a future hire date without requiring termination. Why was this functionality not considered in Onboarding 2.0? What was previously an automated process has now become highly manual.

entry.title: Re: IC Stock Transfer Returns Process w/ outbound and inbound delivery (Private Cloud / On Prem)
entry.content: Any possibility we can add line Qty /Date manually into Inbound delivery. I just copied 0004 into Z004.  After creating PO - confirmation control Line is getting disabled in my case case.

entry.title: Re: Contingent Worker/External to Employee Conversions th...
entry.content:
@tracymca 

This is not a viable solution for us, and likely not for others as well. Our contingent workers typically end their assignment on Friday and return on Monday as employees. We enter the contingent worker termination on Sunday to ensure they do not lose IT access during the transition to employee status. It is business critical they do not lose access.

Even if we were to terminate them on Saturday, that would leave only one day for HR to initiate onboarding, for the contingent worker to complete their tasks, and for HR to finalize onboarding as a new hire. During this gap, the worker would lose access and it's not feasible to complete all tasks in one day.

In Onboarding 1.0, the system recognized future‑dated terminations and allowed workers to proceed with onboarding using a future termination date as long as the hire date was after the termination date.  Why was this functionality not considered in Onboarding 2.0?

What was previously an automated process has now become highly manual.

entry.title: Unable to Find Learning Modules for C_TS462 – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sales

I am preparing for the certification C_TS462 – SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sales.

However, I am currently unable to find the official learning modules/course content related to this certification in SAP Learning Hub or the SAP Learning site.

Could anyone please guide me on:

  1. Where I can find the official learning journey or course modules for C_TS462?

  2. Whether the training is available as self-paced learning or only as instructor-led/live sessions?

  3. If there are specific course codes (e.g., TS462 or related courses) that I should enroll in?

I would appreciate any guidance or official links that can help me proceed with my preparation.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Pragathi S

entry.title: MDG_BS_SUPPLIERREPLICATEREQ service group not available in SOAMANAGER
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are configuring SOAMANAGER for Business Partner by following the below link - 

Configuring SOA Manager for Business Partner | SAP Help Portal

We are stuck on step 6d where we need to Select service groups and provider IBC reference. The mentioned service groups - MDG_BS_SUPPLIERREPLICATEREQ and MDG_BS_SUPPLIERREPLICATECONF are not available and hence we cannot add them and move on.

Anyone faced this issue and can suggest what wrong are we doing - would really appreciate.

Regards,

Pankaj Agarwal

entry.title: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
And with JSON I mean 'canonical' default JSON as per JavaScript, which widely spread and supported in many languages. And not asJSON (ABAP Serialization JSON) which is supported by transformations in ABAP. 

All other de/serializations written in ABAP, like /ui2/cl_json, new one in cloud xco_json, and many open source ones are very slow, and cannot be fast as written in ABAP.

I summarized my idea in improvement request https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/367772, if you think it is relevant for you too, please vote.

entry.title: Re: Delete F4579 configuration
entry.content:
Hello,
BC maintenance objects (R3TR SMBC) can be deleted like any other repository object in ADT.
Just removing the TADIR entry does not delete the object from the relevant table SMBC_CONFIG/SMBC_CONFIG_TEXT. Instead, you can't work with the object anymore. The F4579 will still show it because the relevant CDS view does not care about TADIR, only SMBC_CONFIG. I suggest you restore the TADIR entry and the delete it in ADT.
Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Central Finance Tips and Tricks #5 - SLT
entry.content:
Hi @MarcE 
I have a ongoing issue in SLT. Do you have any idea how to resolve this?

Issue: Filters (Conversion logic) not working.

Scenario:
We have 3 non-SAP Source systems from which the data is being extracted and transformed by a 3rd party ETL tool and then pushed into SLT system and the expectation is that the data would flow onto the CFIN system. However, we have only 2 environments for SLT system (Pre-PROD & PROD). So, the idea is to create 2 Mass Transfer IDs and put Filters (for both Initial Load & Ongoing Replication) to be able to segregate data from Source (DEV) & Source (QAS) and then push it to respective Target CFIN (DEV) & CFIN (QAS) systems. The filters should ensure that Source DEV data gets pushed to Target Dev and Source QAS data gets pushed to Target QAS.

Steps Followed:
1. Created 3 RFCs
A. Pointing from SLT to SLT (RFC_SLT)
B. Pointing from SLT to CFIN DEV (RFC_CFIN_D)
C. Pointing from SLT to CFIN QAS (RFC_CFIN_Q)

2. Created 2 MTID
A. MTID_SLT_CFIN_D [Used RFC_SLT in Source Settings & RFC_CFIN_D in target settings].
B. MTID_SLT_CFIN_Q [Used RFC_SLT in Source Settings & RFC_CFIN_Q in target settings].

3. Create Predefined Load / Replication Objects
A. For Load Object:
Copied from Std. Repl Object (Project: REPL_CFIN, Subproject: REPL_CFIN_ST, Predefined Object: CFIST_ACDOC_L) and used Table /1LT/CF_E_HEADER.
B. For Replication Object:
Copied from Std. Repl Object (Project: REPL_CFIN, Subproject: REPL_CFIN_ST, Predefined Object: CFIST_ACDOC_R) and used Table /1LT/CF_E_HEADER.

4. Add Conversion Logic to Subproject.
Selected Subproject and then created a Conversion Logic by changing the Event-based Rule: SKIP_DOCUMENT. [Logic: If Logical System = SDEV then SKIP_RECORD, SDEV - Logical system for Source DEV system].

5. Generated Runtime Object by completing all the Steps for Runtime Object for Load Object.

6. Attempted to generate Runtime object for Replication Object (but FAILED with error stating that a undefined structure <IT_S> exists). On checking the Function Group generated after attempting to generate runtime objects, this structure exists and is truly undefined.

7. Advance Replication (LTRS) Settings:
Applied Filter Logic [Logical system = SDEV]

We have not setup the MTID for QAS system yet. However, when data is sent for Source DEV & Source QAS to test the FILTERS, data from both the Source system environments get transferred to CFIN DEV which shows that the FILTERS ARE NOT WORKING.

entry.title: How to open cases for end customer as an SAP Partner
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

I hope this email finds you well.

I understand this may not be the correct channel for this inquiry; however, it appears to be the only available channel to submit my question. I kindly ask for your understanding.

Starting with the 2602 update, ByD cases must be created via SAP for Me. To submit a case, a user with the company’s S-ID needs to grant me the appropriate authorization. However, I am currently not aware of the S-ID associated with the company.

Could you please advise on how I may submit a case under this company?

Thank you very much for your assistance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to open cases for end customer as an SAP Partner
entry.content:
Hello,

You may find the "Service Partner User" (SPU) function useful for your issue.

As a partner, you can refer to KBA 2518736 - "Service Partners: How to get authorizations for your S-user ID to create support cases on-behalf of your customers - SAP for Me".

Your customer needs to invite your S-user as written in KBA 2518600 - "How does a customer invite partner S-user IDs to create cases on their behalf using the Service Partner User functionality - SAP for Me".
(It's important to pay special attention to step 6. "Set a validity period for their engagement" or otherwise the functionality will not work.)

If you need any further assistance, you may contact SAP Partner Support as per KBA 1848459 - How do SAP Partners get help with all partner topics and applications (including Partner Dashboards in SAP for Me)

Best regards,
SAP Support

Look at AMAZON Q and how they are enabling people, SAP will of course do the same but when it is quite late like they always do. SAP learn to make things simple not complicated . Till then I am  happy promoting Amazon Q to customer development teams and within the organisation.

entry.title: Compatibity of S/4HANA 2025 SLT with Sidecar HANA

Hi Team,

S/4HANA 2025 SLT with Target HANA DB 2.0 Revision 79 PL08 as SIDECAR. 

Regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Compatibity of S/4HANA 2025 SLT with Sidecar HANA
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

Thanks for contacting SLT support team. 

SLT compatibility with DB/OS depends on Not SLT but NetWeaver. You can check that in PAM, as it is mentioned in KBA 3458950 - DMIS compatibility with DB/OS - SLT

 

Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Where did the BTP ABAP certification (C_ABAPD) go?
entry.content: @RichHeilman , will the learning journey be the same or is that being refined as well?

entry.title: How SAP ESM + Document AI + S/4HANA Work Together to Automate Invoice Processing End‑to‑End
entry.content:
One of the most powerful capabilities in SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) is its ability to automate invoice processing the moment a customer or vendor sends an email. With SAP Document Information Extraction (Document AI) integrated into ESM, the journey from a raw invoice PDF to a validated posting in SAP S/4HANA Private or Public Edition becomes almost fully automated.
 

Step 1: The User Sends an Email with an Invoice

A customer or vendor sends an email to the SAP ESM dedicated email address configured for automatic case creation.

The invoice may be:

  • Attached as a PDF, JPEG, PNG, or
  • Embedded in the email body itself

SAP ESM picks up both.

Step 2: SAP ESM Automatically Creates a Case

Once the email reaches the ESM mailbox:

  • A new case is created automatically
  • The email body and all attachments are stored within the case
  • The attachments (PDFs, images) trigger SAP Document AI processing immediately

This eliminates manual case creation entirely.

Step 3: SAP Document AI Processes the Invoice

SAP Document AI retrieves the attachment from the case and begins extraction using OCR and machine learning.

It identifies:

  • Invoice number
  • Customer or vendor name
  • Address
  • Line items
  • Totals & taxes
  • Payment terms
  • Contract or PO references

Document AI also calculates a confidence score for each extracted field.
These scores help determine whether the data is reliable enough to auto‑post or requires user intervention.

Step 4: Validation Using Business Rules & Customer Framework

Once extraction is complete, SAP ESM applies:

  • Business rules
  • Customer validation frameworks
  • Mapping logic
  • Master data checks

These validations are essential before communicating with S/4HANA.

Examples:

  • Is the customer known in S/4?
  • Is the invoice number valid and not duplicated?
  • Does the PO exist?
  • Do line item totals match?

Only when the validations pass does ESM proceed to the next step.

Step 5: Invoice Number & Customer Auto‑Identification

If Document AI finds the invoice number, SAP ESM updates the line items and case fields:

  • invoiceNumber → updated
  • customerNumber → auto‑identified
  • isClearing → set according to logic (e.g., invoice found, ready to post)

This gives the ESM user immediate visibility:

  • Has the system recognized the invoice?
  • Has the customer been identified?
  • Is the invoice ready for posting?

Step 6: ESM Sends the Validated Invoice to S/4HANA via OData APIs

Once the case has all mandatory and validated data, the ESM user can trigger the next action:

“Post Invoice to S/4”

At this point:

  • ESM calls S/4HANA OData APIs
  • A draft or final FI invoice is created
  • Additional steps like clearing, matching, or workflow approvals occur inside S/4

This hands-off process ensures:

  • No manual entry in S/4
  • No data inconsistencies
  • Full auditability from source → document → posting

Step 7: ESM User Monitors and Completes the Process

Inside the ESM case, the user can now see:

  • Status of the posting
  • Document number returned from S/4
  • Any errors (e.g., master data inconsistencies)
  • New actions available for follow-up

They can then:

  • Update the customer
  • Add missing reference information
  • Re-trigger the posting
  • Close the case once processing is complete

Result: A Fully Streamlined, AI‑Driven Invoice Workflow

By integrating SAP ESM, Document AI, and S/4HANA, organizations benefit from:

✔ Zero manual invoice entry

✔ Auto-identification of customers and invoice data

✔ Early error detection using business rules

✔ Faster posting to S/4HANA

✔ End-to-end transparency in one central case

✔ Higher accuracy and reduced effort

This modernizes invoice handling from a traditionally manual process into an intelligent, automated workflow.

Learning Journey :  Business Documents in ESM

entry.title: Goods Issunce Against Production Order in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

My components storage location is EWM. My Production storage location is IM. I am doing goods issuance against outbound delivery created through MF60 staging of components w.r.t Production order.
When I post goods issue against outbound delivery, movement type 411 is performed automatically which shows stock at Production storage location. 
I have a demand. I want that when i post goods issue against Outbound delivery 313 movement type hits so that till production storage location guys doesn't confirm arrival of goods, it stays in transit.
I am using Public Cloud. Need assistance whether it is possible? If yes, guide me steps so that all time when goods issue is posted, 313 movement type hits.

entry.title: Re: change key fields in custom BO in SAP C4C V1 after tr...
entry.content:
Modifying Alternative Key Fields in SAP C4C Custom Business Objects After Transport
Current Situation Analysis
Based on your CBO definition, you want to remove the [AlternativeKey] annotation from field2 after the business object has already been transported to multiple tenants. This is a significant structural change that requires careful consideration.

Key Limitations for Transported CBOs
Critical Restriction: You cannot modify or delete fields that were transported [1]. This is implemented by design to prevent data loss and maintain system integrity across tenants ,[2].

Specific Limitations Include:

Cannot change field types of transported fields
Cannot modify key field attributes (add/remove AlternativeKey annotation) [3],[2],[4]
Cannot delete transported fields [1],[5]
Cannot change field IDs [6]
Assessment: Regular Patch vs Deletion Patch
Regular Patch Transport: Not Supported

Removing the [AlternativeKey] annotation from field2 constitutes a structural change to the key definition
This type of modification is not allowed through regular patch transport for transported CBOs [1],[2]
Deletion Patch: Required but Limited

A deletion patch would be necessary for this type of structural change [7]
However, deletion patches are primarily designed for removing entire fields or objects, not modifying annotations
Even with deletion patches, changing key field attributes may not be supported
Recommended Solutions
Option 1: Create New Custom Business Object (Recommended)
Process:

Copy the existing CBO using the "Copy" function in the Custom Business Objects app [8],[2]
Modify the new CBO by removing the [AlternativeKey] annotation from field2
Update all dependencies:
Custom Logic references
UI elements
Integration points
Reports and queries
Migrate data from old CBO to new CBO (if needed)
Deactivate the old CBO by removing all UI access and business logic references [2]
Option 2: Add New Field as Alternative Key
Alternative Approach:

Add a new field (e.g., field5) with the [AlternativeKey] annotation
Keep field2 as is (since it cannot be modified)
Update business logic to use the new alternative key field
Gradually phase out usage of field2 as a key in custom logic
Implementation Steps for Option 1
Dependency Analysis

Navigate to Extensibility Inventory from your CBO to check all dependencies [2],[4]
Document all objects that reference the current CBO
Create New CBO

import AP.Common.GDT as apCommonGDT;

businessobject CBO_V2 {
[Label("field1")] [AlternativeKey] element SerialNumber : ID ;
[Label("field2")] element field2 :LANGUAGEINDEPENDENT_MEDIUM_Description; // AlternativeKey removed
[Label("field3")] element field3 :LANGUAGEINDEPENDENT_MEDIUM_Description;
[Label("field4")] element field4 :LANGUAGEINDEPENDENT_MEDIUM_Description;
}
Update Dependencies

Modify all custom logic to reference the new CBO
Update UI elements and forms
Adjust integration scenarios
Data Migration (if required)

Use OData services to extract data from old CBO [9]
Load data into new CBO structure
Risk Mitigation Strategies
Data Preservation:

The old CBO will remain in the system but should be made inaccessible to end users [2]
All business data remains intact in the original structure
Testing Protocol:

Thoroughly test the new CBO in development system
Validate all business processes using the new structure
Ensure query performance meets requirements
Rollback Plan:

Keep the original CBO structure as backup
Document all changes for potential rollback scenarios
Best Practices for Future CBO Design
Alternative Key Planning:

Carefully consider alternative key requirements before initial transport [10]
Design key structures that are unlikely to change
Use UUID-based keys when human-readable keys might change
Transport Strategy:

Plan structural changes before initial transport
Consider creating multiple versions of CBOs for different use cases
Document key field dependencies thoroughly
Conclusion
Unfortunately, there is no direct way to modify the AlternativeKey annotation of field2 in your transported CBO. The recommended approach is to create a new CBO with the desired structure and migrate your business processes to use the new object. While this requires additional effort, it ensures data integrity and system stability across all your tenants.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting 2023 On-Prem: Due to the latest upgra...
entry.content:
Please check SAP for Me and also on this Community site, a number of items are dedicated to  S/4HANA and "Group Reporting" issues when upgrading.

KBA 3446422 - Group Reporting: How to check What's New section in Group Reporting - SAP for Me indicates how / why you should inspect the "What's New" section carefully of a new S/4-version ... Next, see KBA 2371973 - EC-CS in the context of SAP S/4HANA OP ( "on premise" ) - SAP for Me and the following extract related to S/4 2023 OP :

EC-CS in SAP S/4HANA OP 2023

  • For usage of EC-CS in S/4HANA OP 2023, software component S4CORE, release level 108 is required.
  • Mainstream maintenance of EC-CS in SAP S/4HANA OP 2023 will be terminated with the compatibility scope, at end of year 2025.
  • If you are using EC-CS in SAP S/4HANA, you need to migrate to the alternative functionality SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting. See SAP Note 2833748 for details. The license for SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting includes usage right for EC-CS until December 31st, 2027.

The information of KBA 2833748 - SAP S/4HANA OP ( "on premise" ) - Transition from EC-CS to SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting - SAP for Me is extensive and should be analyzed on missing steps when upgrading ...

Finally, KBA 3640892 - Group Reporting: Error pop-up in Data Monitor. System has been upgraded from release S/4HANA 1909. - SAP for Me refers to S/4 1909 but is good as a Solution Proposal. If the mentioned Config does not match with your S/4 2023 OP System, I would suggest referencing the KBA 3640892 in a Support Case to SAP, suggesting to create a similar Guide for S/4 2023 ...

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: I need a process for bulk liquid deliveries with multiple stops per truck.
entry.content: My company will ship a tank truck of product ~5000 gallons or 40000 Lbs. and will stop off at multiple ship to locations e.g. 400 gallons 1st, then 500 gallons 2nd, and so on. We would like to post goods issue the billable qty as per stop off for what they received. Is there a best practice for this request? We are currently creating a delivery and confirm pick quantity as actual and change the sales order to match the delivery quantity then we bill the customer based on each drop's actual quantity. This being a manual process I would like to see if there is a standard process for this scenario.

Cheers,

Rich Heilman

entry.title: From Business Objects to Business Flows: The Missing Layer of RAP Documentation
entry.content:
Part 2 in a series on modern SAP documentation.

Part 1 covered Business Object Canvases, ADRs, and dependency matrices. This post tackles what happens when you need to understand how it all fits together.

In my last post, I argued that RICEFW documentation doesn't fit how we build with RAP. Business Object Canvases, ADRs, and dependency matrices give us a better way to document individual capabilities and the decisions behind them.

But I ended with an open question: what happens when someone asks “Walk me through what happens when a customer creates an order”? You point them to four BO Canvases. They read all four. They still don't understand the sequence — what triggers what, in which order, and what happens when something fails.

Functional Specs Describe Pieces, Not Flows

Our team documents in Confluence today. Functional specifications per RICEFW object — one for the report, another for the interface, another for the enhancement, another for the form. They're organized around technical artifacts, not business capabilities.

So if you want to understand the Sales Order, you're reading across half a dozen specs. The enhancement spec for validation, the interface spec for pricing, the form spec for order confirmation. Each makes sense on its own. But piecing together what actually happens when a customer creates an order means jumping between documents that were never designed to tell that story.

Questions like these are hard to answer:

What happens after the sales order is saved? What events fire, and in what order? If credit check fails, does the order stay blocked, or does it roll back? Which steps are synchronous and which are async? Where does pricing fit — before, during, or after save?

The answers live in people's heads, scattered across specs, or buried in code.

Why RAP Makes This Worse — and Better

In classic ABAP, process orchestration was often implicit. User exits, BAdIs, and function modules wired through transaction codes. You could trace the flow by reading the code.

RAP changes this. Business objects are decoupled. They communicate through events, EML calls, and service projections. Good architecture — but the process flow is no longer visible in any single place. The good news: because RAP forces you to think in events and clear BO boundaries, the sequence can be documented cleanly. You just need to actually do it.

An Idea: Sequence Maps

What I think we need is something simple — a visual flow that shows the chain of BOs in a business process, the trigger between each step, and whether it's synchronous or asynchronous. Nothing fancy. A diagram in Confluence: Customer creates order → Sales Order BO (SAVE) → [sync] Pricing determination → [sync] Credit check → [event] OrderCreated → [async] Inventory reservation → [async] Shipping notification

Even something this basic should be valuable. New developers could see the flow in thirty seconds. Architects could spot the sync/async boundaries. Application experts could validate whether the sequence matches what they expect.

The Full Picture

Each documentation layer answers a different question:

  • BO Canvas: What does this business object do?
  • ADR: Why did we build it this way?
  • Dependency matrix: What's connected to what?
  • Sequence map: What happens when a business process runs?

The sequence mapping is the part I haven't started yet — but it's the gap I feel most in daily work. Too many “go ask Henrik, he knows” conversations. If you're on a similar journey — moving from RICEFW specs to something that fits RAP — I'd like to hear what you've tried.

entry.title: The program RBNK_IMPORT_PAYM_STATUS_REPORT is checking with REGUHM table
entry.content:
We are using F110 to generate the payment file to the bank (ISO20022 – pain.001), and we are now trying to implement the pain.002 payment status report using the report RBNK_IMPORT_PAYM_STATUS_REPORT. However, the report throws the error message:
“Provided document might have invalid information.”

Has anyone encountered this issue before or has an idea on how to resolve it?
Your help is highly appreciated

entry.title: Replicating ASE TDE to heterogenous target (Oracle)
entry.content:
Hi - 

SAP documentation is a bit confusing when discussing replicating encrypted data to a heterogenous target (Oracle).  

Encrypted columns says no heterogenous targets are supported but other documentation may indicate support TDE ASE replication to a heterogenous target.

entry.title: is it possible to delete a sap user from 000 client by logging in from 100 client
Re: CCV2 no column name was specified for column 2 of CNT...
entry.content:
The paginated FlexibleSearch wraps your query into a derived table (the CNTTABLEALIAS you see in the exception) to compute counts / apply pagination. In that derived table, every selected column must have a name.
Your select query:

SELECT item_t0.p_customer , MAX( item_t0.createdTS )

Column #2 is an expression without an alias, so the derived table ends up with “column 2 has no name” → exactly the error:

No column name was specified for column 2 of 'CNTTABLEALIAS'

Fix: alias the 2nd selected column in the outer SELECT

Give the MAX(...) (or any column expression) an alias, e.g. AS maxColumn

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC: Benefits, Architecture and implementation
entry.content:
@CasCriel79

If the goal is to surface data to HANA disk and memory for performance, how does the current replication flow—moving data from FOS object storage to HANA disk—work, given that Replication Flows do not support object stores as a source 3538038 - Important considerations for SAP Datasphere object store - SAP for Me.
I also need to try Delta Sharing to see if it works—perhaps the share for the Standard Data Products is available from Object Storage, which we currently do not have visibility into. 
I hope the SAP team can provide support and clarity on this soon.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud Certificate
entry.content:
Thank you Margit and Lydie. I have done that 3 free course: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-sap-business-data-cloud#sap-certified-sap-business-data-cloud

When I click on Open:

It still prompt me for Subscription. It is because I have not purchase any valid certification attempt yet (i.e. exam with 2 attempt, not full SAP Learning Hub subscription).

Regarding the exam format, I just want to confirm that this is not multiple choice anymore???

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/scenario-based-assessment-faqs#seeking-practical-insights-best-practices-and-hands-on-guidance

And with this new format, it is open book test?

Thanks for your help.
